code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of (subject, predicate, object) triples about a comics character
    into a single fluent English sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from objects
    def clean(obj: str) -> str:
        obj = obj.strip()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            return obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    # Phrase fragments for each predicate (subject will be added separately)
    phrase_map = {
        "alternativeName": "is also known as \"{object}\"",
        "creator": "was created by {object}",
        "fullName": "'s full name is {object}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {object}",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {object}",
        "city": "is based in {object}",
        "product": "produces {object}",
        "lastAired": "last aired on {object}",
        "starring": "starred {object}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": "first appeared in the film {object}",
        "voice": "is voiced by {object}",
        "distributor": "was distributed by {object}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {object}",
        "nationality": "is from {object}",
        "award": "received the {object}",
        "broadcastedBy": "was broadcasted by {object}",
        "firstAired": "first aired on {object}",
        "series": "belongs to the series {object}",
        "child": "has a child named {object}",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject name
    from collections import defaultdict
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        grouped[t.subject].append(t)

    subject_clauses = []
    for subject, tlist in grouped.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                part = tmpl.format(object=clean(t.object))
                # Handle possessive case for fullName (subject already includes name)
                if t.predicate == "fullName":
                    part = f"{subject}{part}"
                else:
                    parts.append(part)
            else:
                # Fallback for unknown predicates
                parts.append(f"{t.predicate} {clean(t.object)}")
        # Build clause for this subject
        if not parts:
            continue
        if len(parts) == 1:
            clause = f"{subject} {parts[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
        subject_clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine subject clauses into one complex sentence
    if not subject_clauses:
        return ""
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(subject_clauses) + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
